page: Vultr vx1-m-4c-32g-240s is a memory-optimized virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It is configured with 4 shared vCPUs based on the AMD EPYC-Turin processor architecture running at 2.0 GHz. The system features 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 240 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing placing in the top 25% at 3056.38. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches top-tier status in the top 10% at 14.95 GB/sec, and single-threaded gzip compression is strong at 33.72 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-threaded performance is more moderate; stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall into the average category, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25% at 29.72 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth and database operations, including Redis SET operations at 6.88 million operations per second, consistently perform within the average range.

The shared CPU allocation presents a performance tradeoff for sustained multi-threaded compute workloads, as reflected in the lower multi-threaded decompression benchmarks. However, the high memory density relative to the vCPU count provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. This hardware profile is suitable for memory-intensive applications, caching layers, relational databases, and lightweight web serving.
description: Vultr vx1-m-4c-32g-240s is a memory-optimized virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 4 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a 240 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this instance delivers a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance and single-threaded decompression scoring in the top 25% and top 10% respectively. Conversely, multi-threaded performance is average to weak due to the shared CPU allocation. This resource profile offers a cost-efficient solution for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand continuous, dedicated CPU performance. It is well-suited for database operations, caching layers, and general-purpose applications requiring a larger memory footprint.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-4c-32g-240s is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 240 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong single-core performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-4c-32g-240s is a memory-optimized virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 240 GB NVMe SSD, designed for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high memory density and solid single-core performance for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory
Bundled 240 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
High memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU
Best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive tasks
